---
title: "The Wednesday Waggle"
subtitle: For opportunities in Computing and AI
---

The Wednesday Waggle is an email newsletter published every Wednesday [during term time](https://www.manchester.ac.uk/about/key-dates/) summarising opportunities in Computing and AI for:

* all undergraduate students (Bachelors) in Computer Science at the University of Manchester, circa 1000+ in total
* all postgraduate taught (Masters) students in Computer Science at the University of Manchester, circa 240+ in total

The Waggle also gets published during non-teaching periods, although less frequently. Search your email inbox for `Waggle`. The newsletter is edited by [Duncan Hull](https://personalpages.manchester.ac.uk/staff/duncan.hull/), if there is a career related event or a job opportunity you'd like to tell other students about via the next Waggle, tell us about it and we'll pass it on [bit.ly/waggle-it](https://bit.ly/waggle-it).[^1]

![Wednesday Waggle Bee artwork by [Visual Thinkery](https://visualthinkery.com/) is licensed under [CC-BY-ND](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nd/4.0/)](Wednesday_Waggle.png)


## Not studying CS? Add yourself to the mailing list {#waggle-me}

If you're studying a subject other than Computer Science (CS) at the University of Manchester, but have an interest in opportunities in Computing and AI, you can add yourself to the Wednesday Waggle mailing list at [bit.ly/waggle-me](https://bit.ly/waggle-me).^[1]

## Employers: Add your vacancies etc {#employers}

If you're an employer who'd like to include a vacancy, event or opportunity to the next newsletter, tell us about it using this form: [forms.cloud.microsoft/e/1y4XTERggU](https://forms.cloud.microsoft/e/1y4XTERggU)

If you're interested in reaching students beyond Computer Science, see [employers.manchester.ac.uk](https://www.employers.manchester.ac.uk)

## The Wednesday What? {#wtf}

The Wednesday What? The Wednesday [Waggle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waggle_dance). Bees have been a [Mancunian icon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Symbols_of_Manchester) since the industrial revolution, symbolising community (the hive) and industry (work ethic). Just like the waggle dance of bees tells the hive mind where the [nectar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nectar), [pollen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pollen) and other resources are located, this newsletter aims to help students like you find promising opportunities for investigation. 🐝


[^1]: bit.ly links redirects to a Microsoft form requiring you to login using your UoM credentials.
